title: Document Freedom Day
description: Document Freedom Day celebrates open document standards — the right of everyone to read, write, and share documents using the tools of their choice, free from lock-in. Led by the Free Software Foundation Europe.

title: Hardware Freedom Day
description: Hardware Freedom Day celebrates free and open source hardware — designs that anyone can study, modify, distribute, and manufacture. It's a global movement to make the physical world as open as the software world.

title: International Timebanking Day
description: International Timebanking Day celebrates timebanking — a means of exchange where time is the currency. Everyone's hour is equal. Communities use timebanking to build reciprocity, mutual aid, and alternative economies grounded in human connection.

title: Linux Birthday
description: On 25 August 1991, Linus Torvalds announced a 'free operating system (just a hobby, won't be big and professional like gnu)'. That hobby now powers the world. Linux Birthday celebrates over three decades of open source collaboration and the commons it created.
dateRuleText: August 25 — the anniversary of Linus's announcement

title: Software Freedom Day
description: Software Freedom Day is a worldwide celebration of free and open source software. Communities around the world hold local events to educate the public about the benefits of free software and free software's role in a free society.
